rationale: In the citing paper, this source is cited in the data and measurement sections, not just in a generic literature list. The authors rely on it (alongside Simpson 2008) to justify key empirical setup choices: which firms FRR44 applies to and how disclosure is coded from accounting fields. Those choices are central to the paper's identification strategy and dependent variable construction, so the citation plays a meaningful functional role. At the same time, the citing paper does not build on the cited paper's main theory about strategy-moderated advertising effectiveness.
relation_description: The citing paper uses McAlister et al. (2016) as a methodological precedent for the FRR44 disclosure setting and for coding advertising disclosure from Compustat. It applies these prior operationalization choices to study peer herding in disclosure decisions rather than advertising effectiveness outcomes.
